reason: Hardware device family model. The RC2 Layer 1 registry covers software entities only across five categories: Driver, Firmware, Operating System, Security, and Management. Hardware device platforms and laptop/workstation models are structurally out of scope for the Layer 1 software entity registry. No registration action is required or appropriate.
suggested_action: No Layer 1 action required. This entity's role in compatibility rules is as an Exception exclusion scope only — device families exempt from a rule. Document as hardware platform scope reference if needed.

reason: Hardware device family model. Same out-of-scope classification as 'ProBook and Enterprise Laptop Series'. The Layer 1 RC2 registry does not and should not register hardware device platforms.
suggested_action: No Layer 1 action required. This entity's role in compatibility rules is as a Condition scope (hardware context in which the rule applies) and Exception scope. Document as hardware platform scope reference if needed.

reason: The SIEM platform concept was resolved to MGT-008 (SIEM). The third-party connector software itself — certified under the ACME Partner Program (vendor='ACME') — does not have a Layer 1 equivalent. Third-party integration software from external partners is outside the scope of the Layer 1 software entity registry which covers only generic, vendor-neutral domain components.
suggested_action: No Layer 1 action required. SIEM platform reference is resolved to MGT-008. The ACME Partner Program and third-party connector software are outside Layer 1 scope. If third-party integration tracking is required it should be addressed in a separate partner/integration registry.
